title: Design a Model Rocket
description: Design, simulate, and (optionally) build a model rocket using OpenRocket. Apply the Tsiolkovsky equation, select motor specifications, optimise fin geometry, and predict altitude and recovery.
whyItMatters: Model rocketry bridges theory and practice. Every concept — thrust curves, drag, stability margins, centre of pressure vs centre of gravity — applies directly to full-scale launch vehicles. NASA engineers at Huntsville still build model rockets to test concepts.

steps.title: Define requirements
steps.description: Set a target altitude (e.g., 300 metres), maximum diameter, and recovery method (parachute). Select a commercially available motor class (e.g., Estes C6-5).
steps.tips: Start with a simple single-stage design. Check local regulations for model rocketry in your state.

steps.title: Preliminary design in OpenRocket
steps.description: Model the nose cone, body tube, fins, and motor mount. Set materials and dimensions. Run a stability check — ensure at least 1.5 calibres of stability margin.
steps.tips: Centre of pressure must be behind centre of gravity by at least 1.5 body diameters.

steps.title: Simulate and optimise
steps.description: Run flight simulations in OpenRocket. Vary fin size, nose cone shape, and motor selection to maximise altitude while maintaining stability.
steps.tips: Watch the thrust-to-weight ratio at liftoff — must exceed 5:1 for safe launch.

steps.title: Apply the rocket equation
steps.description: Calculate theoretical delta-v from the motor specifications and verify against OpenRocket's simulation. Account for gravity and drag losses.
steps.tips: Motor total impulse and propellant mass give you the numbers for Tsiolkovsky.

steps.title: Document the design
steps.description: Create a design report with drawings, simulation results, mass budget, and stability analysis. If possible, build and fly the rocket, comparing actual altitude with predictions.
steps.tips: Take video of the flight for later analysis. A phone app altimeter can verify peak altitude.

deliverables: Simulation results with altitude, velocity, and acceleration plots

rubric.description: All components specified with materials, dimensions, and masses
Static stability margin between 1.5 and 3.0 calibres
Clear design report that could be followed by another engineer
